Article: End of Evangelion Kiyoshi Kawakamis 1997 translation
[]: End of Evangelion: Kiyoshi Kawakami's 1997 translation             Quote Post by gwern » Fri Aug 26, 2011 9:40 pm This is the script that Patrick Yip/symbv was originally drawing upon when he was translating EoE (http://eva.onegeek.org/pipermail/oldeva/1998-January/008787.html), although he says in a later email he didn't use too much of it. Kawakami is not very good at English, but it's always nice to have another EoE translation to go with 2000A and Yasuyuki Sato's 1998 translation (http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=11401). I've only skimmed it, looking at the Komm Susser Tod scene in particular; it does seem to have a lot of problems, but the literalness may be of use sometimes. The links: 25': http://www.fortunecity.com/rivendell/krondor/502/air.txt 26': http://www.fortunecity.com/rivendell/krondor/502/mphfy.txt As near as I can determine, these are Kawakami's final revisions. (There are one or two preliminary revisions floating around.)

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Fri Aug 26, 2011 9:47 pm Seems the Fortune City links don't work.

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Fri Aug 26, 2011 9:50 pm They work fine; it seems that fortunecity.com is checking referrers and quite evilly blocking them. Copy them into the address bar and they work fine. (I had no problem wgetting them, for example.)

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Fri Aug 26, 2011 10:50 pm gwern wrote: They work fine; it seems that fortunecity.com is checking referrers and quite evilly blocking them. Copy them into the address bar and they work fine. (I had no problem wgetting them, for example.)

Article: End of Evangelion Kiyoshi Kawakamis 1997 translation
:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sat Aug 27, 2011 12:37 pm Compared to the other translations out there, this one isn't very useful. I find that when translating, it's a lot more important to have the translator natively speak the language they're translating into, rather than in the other way around.

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Sat Aug 27, 2011 12:44 pm chaosakita wrote: I find that when translating, it's a lot more important to have the translator natively speak the language they're translating into, rather than in the other way around.
